channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes. How will this affect deal to end war?
item.description: Do new strikes affect a potential deal to end the war with Iran? NPR's Scott Detrow breaks it down with NPR International Correspondent Aya Batrawy and NPR Pentagon Correspondent Tom Bowman.
item.content:encoded: Do new strikes affect a potential deal to end the war with Iran? NPR's Scott Detrow breaks it down with NPR International Correspondent Aya Batrawy and NPR Pentagon Correspondent Tom Bowman.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: The possible return of Somali pirates poses a new problem for the shipping industry
item.description: As the global economy focuses on the Strait of Hormuz crisis, another shipping crisis looms in the region — the return of Somali pirates.
item.content:encoded: As the global economy focuses on the Strait of Hormuz crisis, another shipping crisis looms in the region — the return of Somali pirates.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Australian woman charged over travel to Syria to join Islamic State
item.description: The arrest came eight months after she returned to Australia with her children from Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
The arrest came eight months after she returned to Australia with her children from Lebanon.

(Image credit: Baderkhan Ahmad)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Orthodox Jewish women in Israel may now take a rabbinic exam, like men
item.description: Until now, only Orthodox Jewish men in Israel have been allowed to take exams to become rabbis. After a long campaign, women can now also take the test - but still can't become rabbis
item.content:encoded:
Until now, only Orthodox Jewish men in Israel have been allowed to take exams to become rabbis. After a long campaign, women can now also take the test - but still can't become rabbis

(Image credit: Ofir Berman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran again. And, DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades
item.description: The U.S. says it has struck Iran again as peace talks continue to end the conflict. And, the federal government has charged a Google staffer for allegedly using insider information in Polymarket trades.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. says it has struck Iran again as peace talks continue to end the conflict. And, the federal government has charged a Google staffer for allegedly using insider information in Polymarket trades.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: More than 500 people have died in Bangladesh measles outbreak
item.description: Bangladesh is scrambling to vaccinate more children amid a measles outbreak that has killed more than 500 people, most of them children.
item.content:encoded: Bangladesh is scrambling to vaccinate more children amid a measles outbreak that has killed more than 500 people, most of them children.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: In Colombia, a flashy lawyer touts his tough-on-crime approach in presidential race
item.description: In Colombia's election this Sunday, a flamboyant criminal lawyer — who flaunts his wealth, machismo and even his music videos — is pitching himself as the toughest candidate on crime.
item.content:encoded: In Colombia's election this Sunday, a flamboyant criminal lawyer — who flaunts his wealth, machismo and even his music videos — is pitching himself as the toughest candidate on crime.
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak is spiraling, with health workers struggling to contain the virus
item.description: The epicenter of the Ebola outbreak is in Mongbwalu, a poor gold-mining town of 130,000 people, in Ituri province, in eastern Congo.
item.content:encoded:
The epicenter of the Ebola outbreak is in Mongbwalu, a poor gold-mining town of 130,000 people, in Ituri province, in eastern Congo.

(Image credit: Michel Lunanga)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: The possible return of Somali pirates poses a new problem for the shipping industry
item.description: As the global economy focuses on the Strait of Hormuz crisis, another shipping crisis looms in the region -- the return of Somali pirates.
item.content:encoded: As the global economy focuses on the Strait of Hormuz crisis, another shipping crisis looms in the region -- the return of Somali pirates.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Iranians are back online after a monthslong shutdown but face heavy restrictions
item.description: Iranians began to regain internet access after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. Users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted.
item.content:encoded:
Iranians began to regain internet access after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. Users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
